title: Basic Use of Statamarkdown
text: first code chunk look something like : either report Stata found, need specify location . can hide appear final document using include=FALSE chunk options.
code: ```{r library} library(Statamarkdown) ``` library(Statamarkdown) ## Stata found at /Applications/StataNow/StataMP.app/Contents/MacOS/StataMP ## The 'stata' engine is ready to use.

title: Basic Use of Statamarkdown
text: need specify , additional lines "library" code block . know find Stata executable (app), open Stata issue command sysdir. line labeled STATA: folder Stata executable located. can browse computer's file explorer see actual file name Stata executable, varies operating system, Stata version, Stata flavor. make Stata executable path default chunk option.
code: stataexe <- "C:/Program Files/Stata18/StataSE-64.exe" # Windows # stataexe <- "/Applications/Stata/StataSE.app/Contents/MacOS/StataSE" # Mac OS # stataexe <- "/usr/local/stata18/stata-se" # Unix knitr::opts_chunk$set(engine.path = list(stata = stataexe))

title: Stata Markdown
text: collection R functions extends knitr’s capability using Stata language engine. use also Stata installed. can install R package CRAN: GitHub: can check installation package installed, see example created temporary directory. Additional documentation can found https://www.ssc.wisc.edu/~hemken/Stataworkshops/Statamarkdown/stata--r-markdown.html . like contribute project, please “fork” GitHub clone back computer. Make changes enhancements, push back GitHub repository, initiate “pull” request. also welcome open issues, email directly.

title: A helper function that seeks to locate your Stata executable. Ordinarily this is run automatically when Statamarkdown is loaded. — find_stata
code: indoc <- ' # An R console example ## In a first code chunk, set up with ```{r} library(Statamarkdown) ```  ## Then mark Stata code chunks with ```{stata} sysuse auto, clear generate gpm = 1/mpg summarize price gpm ``` '  if (nzchar(Statamarkdown::find_stata())) {   # To run this example, remove tempdir().   fmd <- file.path(tempdir(), "test.md")   fhtml <- file.path(tempdir(), "test.html")    knitr::knit(text=indoc, output=fmd)   rmarkdown::render(fmd, "html_document", fhtml) } #> Stata found at /Applications/StataNow/StataMP.app/Contents/MacOS/StataMP #> 1/5                   #> 2/5 [unnamed-chunk-1] #> 3/5                   #> 4/5 [unnamed-chunk-2] #> 5/5                   #> /opt/homebrew/bin/pandoc +RTS -K512m -RTS test.md --to html4 --from markdown+autolink_bare_uris+tex_math_single_backslash --output /var/folders/kt/7xskrb1n4_x2jkd9lt0rhybh0000gp/T//Rtmp7tOL6T/test.html --lua-filter /Users/eptmp/Library/R/arm64/4.6/library/rmarkdown/rmarkdown/lua/pagebreak.lua --lua-filter /Users/eptmp/Library/R/arm64/4.6/library/rmarkdown/rmarkdown/lua/latex-div.lua --lua-filter /Users/eptmp/Library/R/arm64/4.6/library/rmarkdown/rmarkdown/lua/table-classes.lua --embed-resources --standalone --variable bs3=TRUE --section-divs --template /Users/eptmp/Library/R/arm64/4.6/library/rmarkdown/rmd/h/default.html --syntax-highlighting none --variable highlightjs=1 --variable theme=bootstrap --mathjax --variable 'mathjax-url=https://mathjax.rstudio.com/latest/MathJax.js?config=TeX-AMS-MML_HTMLorMML' --include-in-header /var/folders/kt/7xskrb1n4_x2jkd9lt0rhybh0000gp/T//Rtmp7tOL6T/rmarkdown-str915a5fe9b5ef.html  #>  #> Output created: /var/folders/kt/7xskrb1n4_x2jkd9lt0rhybh0000gp/T//Rtmp7tOL6T/test.html

title: Convert a specially marked up Stata
text: function takes Stata file containing special markup comments, converts Markdown HTML documents (one several formats).

title: Convert a specially marked up Stata
Convert a specially marked up Stata
text: statafile character string name Stata "" file, containing markup comments. text character string place file. keep Whether save intermediate files. ... options passed knitr::spin

title: Convert a specially marked up Stata
text: function takes Stata file containing special markup comments, converts knitr's "spin" format. turn sent knitr::spin, converted Markdown HTML (one several formats). Special Markup: "/*' " - Begin document text, ends "'*/" "/*+ " - Begin chunk header, ends "+*/" "/*R " - Begin chunk R code, ends "R*/" "/** " - Dropped document, ends "*/*"

title: Convert a specially marked up Stata
text: path output file. given text instead file, returns compiled document character string.

title: Convert a specially marked up Stata
text: Doug Hemken

title: Convert a specially marked up Stata
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ indoc <- "/*' # Statamarkdown Example  This is a special Stata script which can be used to generate a report. You can write normal text in command-style comments.  First we load Statamarkdown. '*/    /*+  setup +*/   /*R library(Statamarkdown) R*/    /*' The report begins here. '*/    /*+  example1, engine='stata' +*/   sysuse auto /* Stata comment */   summarize  /*' You can use the ***usual*** Markdown to mark up text.'*/ " if (nzchar(Statamarkdown::find_stata())) {   # To run this example, remove tempdir().   fhtml <- file.path(tempdir(), "test.html")   x<-Statamarkdown::spinstata(text=indoc)   writeLines(x, fhtml) } } # }

title: Statamarkdown 0.9.7
text: CRAN release: 2026-07-19 Fixed loss chunk output Stata log contain “end -file” line. spinstata() longer overwrites input file; statafile without .extension now error, extension check case-insensitive. Fixed crash collectcode chunk option engine.path given plain string rather list. cleanup temporary profile.created collectcode now locates knitr::knit() call call stack instead assuming hardcoded stack depth, making robust changes knitr’s internals, child documents, different ways invoking knitr. cleanlog=TRUE now removes lines multi-line loop commands wrapped (continued) command lines output. cleanlog=TRUE longer deletes genuine output lines begin decimal point. Fixed spinstata() block tracking marked-block starts ends line. Fixed error spinstata() R >= 4.3 producing Rnw Rtex output. find_stata() Windows now stops searching soon executable found, non-existent “StataNow19” directory stub removed search. Removed duplicated “Stata executable found” startup message attaching package without Stata installed. Minor performance improvements: Stata executable located package load, per-chunk processing avoids repeated system calls redundant vector allocations. Change maintainer Tom Palmer. Many thanks Doug Hemken creating maintaining amazing package.
